findings.title: Empty config.yaml causes cryptic crash on startup
findings.description: yaml.safe_load returns None on empty config.yaml, causing AttributeError in validate_required_sections. CLAUDE.md documents config.yaml has been accidentally wiped 3 times.
findings.analysis: Robustness agent traced the load_config path and identified that yaml.safe_load returns None for empty files, which flows unchecked to validate_required_sections where cfg.get() raises AttributeError.
findings.suggested_approach: Add a None check after yaml.safe_load: if cfg is None: raise ValueError('Config file is empty or contains only comments: ' + config_path).

findings.title: call_claude assumes response.content[0] exists without bounds check
findings.description: response.content[0] accessed without checking for empty content list. Anthropic API can return empty content in edge cases. Cost recording happens after this line, so an IndexError loses cost data.
findings.analysis: Robustness agent identified that record_cost is called after content parsing at line 377, meaning an IndexError at line 366 causes both a confusing error and lost cost data.
findings.suggested_approach: Add guard: if not response.content: raise RuntimeError(...). Move record_cost before content parsing to ensure costs are always tracked.

findings.title: resolve_detection does not validate resolution value against allowed set
findings.description: resolve_detection accepts arbitrary string for resolution parameter written directly to pipeline_detections.status column. Invalid values silently remove detections from the pending queue.
findings.analysis: Robustness agent compared resolve_detection (no validation) with update_pipeline_status (validates against frozenset) in the same file, identifying the inconsistency.
findings.suggested_approach: Add validation: if resolution not in ('confirmed', 'dismissed'): raise ValueError(f'Invalid resolution: {resolution!r}').

findings.title: call_claude JSON fallback silently produces wrong-shaped result for structured output callers
findings.description: When API response text cannot be parsed as JSON, fallback creates {'text': str(text)} which has wrong shape for structured output callers. Downstream code gets score=0 persisted as a real Haiku score.
findings.analysis: Robustness agent traced the fallback dict through haiku_scorer (result.get('score') returns None) to pipeline_runner (result.data.get('score', 0) returns 0) showing how a valid-looking but incorrect score gets persisted.
findings.suggested_approach: When output_schema is not None and response is not structured output, raise ValueError rather than falling back to text dict. The fallback is only appropriate for unstructured calls.

findings.title: Pipeline runner duplicates scoring orchestrator instead of delegating to it
findings.description: All six analysis agents independently identified this as the dominant structural debt. scoring_orchestrator was created to centralize Haiku/Sonnet scoring, but pipeline_runner -- the highest-volume consumer -- was never migrated. It contains its own inline scoring loop with raw SQL UPDATEs, borderline re-evaluation, and profile loading.
findings.analysis: Complexity flagged redundant logic and Rube Goldberg pattern. Language flagged duplicated scoring+persistence. API flagged leaky abstraction. Flow traced two divergent codepaths for the same operation. Design flagged module boundary violation and architecture-fit gap. All six agents converged on the same root issue from different perspectives.
findings.suggested_approach: Refactor _run_haiku_scoring to call scoring_orchestrator.score_and_persist_haiku() and _run_sonnet_evaluation to call score_and_persist_sonnet(). Pipeline_runner retains enrichment, exclusion filtering, batch iteration, and notification. Eliminates ~100 lines of duplicate logic.

findings.title: Profile loading duplicated across four modules with divergent path resolution
findings.description: Four independent _load_profile implementations with different path resolution, error handling, and fallback behavior. The scoring_orchestrator version is canonical but pipeline_runner uses its own raw json.load without schema validation.
findings.analysis: Complexity flagged 4 redundant implementations. API flagged naming inconsistency between loaders. Flow traced divergent profile loading paths. Design flagged missing abstraction.
findings.suggested_approach: Replace all three private _load_profile implementations with calls to scoring_orchestrator.load_scoring_profile(config). For interview_prep, add a config parameter.

findings.title: Background job entry points have inconsistent (config, db_path) vs (db_path, config) parameter ordering
findings.description: Two of 8 run_* functions use (config, db_path) while 6 use (db_path, config), forcing adapter lambdas in scheduler.py. Every new background job must navigate this inconsistency.
findings.analysis: API agent identified the inconsistency by comparing all run_* signatures and tracing the adapter lambdas in scheduler.py that work around it.
findings.suggested_approach: Standardize all to (db_path: str, config: dict) which is the majority convention. Update run_ingestion and run_pipeline_detection, remove adapter lambdas.

findings.title: ats_scanner imports private functions from pipeline_runner creating bidirectional dependency
findings.description: ats_scanner imports _run_haiku_scoring and _run_sonnet_evaluation (underscore-prefixed private functions) from pipeline_runner, creating bidirectional dependency. Both modules are harder to refactor independently.
findings.analysis: Language agent identified the cross-module private function imports and traced the bidirectional dependency between pipeline_runner and ats_scanner.
findings.suggested_approach: Extract scoring entry points into scoring_orchestrator. Have ats_scanner import from scoring_orchestrator instead. This breaks the bidirectional dependency.

findings.title: Mixed ScoringResult and legacy dict handling in scoring orchestrator
findings.description: score_and_persist_haiku and score_and_persist_sonnet use hasattr duck-typing to handle both ScoringResult and plain dict returns. The dual-type support exists for legacy/mocked callers but all production functions now return ScoringResult. The unwrap pattern is duplicated between both functions.
findings.analysis: Robustness flagged the mixed error return conventions. API flagged the unclear contract. Design flagged the duplicated unwrap pattern suggesting a missing shared helper.
findings.suggested_approach: Remove the legacy dict path. Add unwrap_scoring_result() helper to scoring_types.py. Update test mocks to return ScoringResult. Makes contract unambiguous.

findings.title: Scoring evaluator and backfill scripts duplicate score persistence SQL
findings.description: scoring_evaluator.py and backfill_enrichment.py write Haiku/Sonnet scores via inline SQL, bypassing db.persist_haiku_score and db.persist_sonnet_score.
findings.analysis: Flow agent traced the score persistence paths and identified these as additional codepaths that bypass the canonical persistence layer.
findings.suggested_approach: Import and use db.persist_haiku_score and db.persist_sonnet_score instead of inline SQL.

findings.title: Bare except Exception silently swallows errors in dashboard and db queries
findings.description: Three db.py functions catch bare except Exception returning empty defaults. Intended for pre-migration graceful degradation but also catches SQL bugs and connection failures. Dashboard _update_session_counter uses f-string SQL interpolation without allowlist validation.
findings.analysis: Language flagged broad exception catches in critical paths. Robustness flagged the same pattern masking production errors. API flagged callers unable to distinguish empty data from query failure.
findings.suggested_approach: Catch sqlite3.OperationalError specifically rather than Exception. Add allowlist check in _update_session_counter.

findings.title: Inconsistent naive vs UTC datetime.now() for database timestamps
findings.description: Codebase mixes datetime.now() (local time) and datetime.now(timezone.utc) for timestamps in the same database. The UTC pattern appears 15+ times; naive local time 18+ times. Cost timestamps are UTC but pipeline timestamps are local time.
findings.analysis: Language agent identified the timezone inconsistency across modules. Complexity agent flagged the repeated verbose UTC incantation and timestamp convention inconsistency.
findings.suggested_approach: Create utc_now_iso() utility function. Standardize all timestamp sites to UTC. Audit datetime.now().isoformat() sites.

findings.title: Description merge logic duplicated between db.py and dedup_normalizer.py
findings.description: Two implementations of the same description merge algorithm with subtly different implementations. Bug fixes in one don't apply to the other.
findings.analysis: Complexity agent compared the two merge implementations and identified divergent logic despite identical semantic intent.
findings.suggested_approach: Refactor _merge_descriptions in dedup_normalizer to call db._merge_description iteratively, or extract into shared utility.

findings.title: Meta-email detection duplicated identically across all four parsers
findings.description: _is_meta_email function and _META_PATTERNS list copy-pasted across all four parsers. Three share identical 4-pattern lists; LinkedIn has one extra. Function bodies identical. Changes must be replicated in four places.
findings.analysis: API agent flagged the naming duplication. Design agent identified both the data clump smell and the missing shared parser base abstraction.
findings.suggested_approach: Extract into parsers/_common.py: META_PATTERNS as base set and is_meta_email(body, extra_patterns=None). LinkedIn passes extra pattern. Indeed keeps custom version.

findings.title: Salary parsing logic duplicated across all four parsers
findings.description: Salary extraction functions with near-identical regex patterns and K-notation conversion logic duplicated across all four parsers.
findings.analysis: Design agent identified the data clump: identical regex, identical K-to-full conversion, identical return types across four independent implementations.
findings.suggested_approach: Extract shared parse_salary_range(text) into parsers/_common.py. Indeed composes with hourly rate fallback.

findings.title: db.py imports from web.blueprints creating upward dependency in layer architecture
findings.description: Persistence layer (db.py) imports VALID_PIPELINE_STATUSES from web.blueprints.__init__, creating a dependency from foundation layer upward into web layer.
findings.analysis: Design agent identified the layer violation and verified VALID_PIPELINE_STATUSES is pure data with no web framework dependency.
findings.suggested_approach: Move PIPELINE_STATUSES and VALID_PIPELINE_STATUSES to job_finder/constants.py at the foundation layer.

findings.title: db.py serves too many disparate consumers from a single 747-line module
findings.description: db.py contains functions spanning five distinct domain concerns: job CRUD, pipeline state, dashboard aggregations, detection resolution, and activity queries. No single consumer uses more than half the module's exports.
findings.analysis: Design agent assessed module cohesion as medium, tracing import patterns to show each blueprint uses a focused subset of db.py's 20+ functions.
findings.suggested_approach: As complexity grows, extract query groups into sub-modules: db_jobs.py, db_pipeline.py, db_dashboard.py. Low priority for single-developer project.

findings.title: db_migrate.py mixes schema migration with one-time operational backfills
findings.description: Schema migration (deterministic, version-based) mixed with one-time operational backfills that spawn background threads and call external APIs. Different failure modes and lifecycle characteristics.
findings.analysis: Design agent identified the mixed responsibilities and traced the different guard conditions, failure modes, and invocation patterns.
findings.suggested_approach: Extract backfill functions into web/startup_backfills.py. Schema migrations stay in db_migrate.py.

findings.title: GmailSource._search_messages has no error handling for pagination failures
findings.description: Gmail API pagination loop has no error handling. Transient error on second page loses all results from first page.
findings.analysis: Robustness agent traced the pagination flow and identified that partial results are lost on API failure.
findings.suggested_approach: Wrap individual API call in try/except that breaks loop on error but returns messages collected so far.

findings.title: _mark_session_error opens connection without try/finally cleanup
findings.description: Sequential conn.execute/conn.commit/conn.close without try/finally. Exception in execute or commit leaves connection unclosed. Windows file locks make this impactful.
findings.analysis: Complexity agent compared this function with other connection sites in the same file that correctly use try/finally.
findings.suggested_approach: Wrap in try/finally: conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path); try: ...; finally: conn.close().

findings.title: SELECT * usage contradicts project's own documented convention (30+ queries)
findings.description: db.py documents 'Explicit column lists for high-traffic queries' but SELECT * appears in 30+ queries. db.py provides _JOBS_ALL_COLUMNS constant that is underutilized.
findings.analysis: Language agent identified 4 specific SELECT * instances and the broader pattern of 30+ occurrences contradicting the project's own documented convention.
findings.suggested_approach: Replace SELECT * with explicit column lists in high-traffic queries. Use _JOBS_ALL_COLUMNS constant or query-specific column lists.

findings.title: call_claude takes 11 parameters spanning four distinct concerns
findings.description: Highest-traffic API takes 11 parameters spanning API setup, cost recording, budget gating, and structured output. Every caller assembles the same (client, conn, config) triple.
findings.analysis: API agent identified 20+ call sites all threading the same invariant triple, with parameter count creating cognitive load.
findings.suggested_approach: Create ClaudeContext dataclass bundling (client, conn, config). Reduces to 8 parameters and eliminates repeated threading.

findings.title: Job dataclass allows empty title/company producing degenerate dedup_key
findings.description: Job dataclass accepts empty strings for title/company. Empty both produces degenerate dedup_key causing all such jobs to collide in database.
findings.analysis: Robustness agent traced dedup_key computation and identified that empty inputs produce collision-prone keys, with parsers checking independently but no boundary enforcement.
findings.suggested_approach: Add __post_init__ validation: if not title.strip() or not company.strip(): raise ValueError.

findings.title: Module-level _last_budget_pct_notified lacks thread safety
findings.description: Module-level float read and written without thread safety in _check_budget_alert. Accessible from both APScheduler thread and Flask request thread via trigger_sync. Unlike _NOTIFY_SEEN, has no lock guard.
findings.analysis: Flow agent traced both entry paths (scheduled run and trigger_sync) to _check_budget_alert and compared thread safety patterns with _NOTIFY_SEEN and _scheduler.
findings.suggested_approach: Add threading.Lock guard following the same pattern as notifier.py's _NOTIFY_LOCK.

findings.title: run.py loads config twice -- once at module level and once in create_app
findings.description: config.yaml parsed twice on startup. Server settings and app config come from different parse results. create_app already accepts config= parameter.
findings.analysis: Robustness and design agents independently identified the double-load, noting create_app's config= parameter already supports single-load pattern.
findings.suggested_approach: Pass config to create_app: cfg = load_config(); app = create_app(config=cfg).

findings.title: Mixed Optional[T] and T | None syntax across 20+ modules
findings.description: Python 3.13 project mixes Optional[T] (20 modules) and T | None (several modules) creating inconsistent style.
findings.analysis: Language agent surveyed type annotation patterns across all Python files and identified the inconsistency.
findings.suggested_approach: Gradually migrate Optional[T] to T | None as files are touched.

findings.title: Redundant imports: sqlite3, datetime alias, logging inside functions, json alias
findings.description: Several import hygiene issues: redundant sqlite3 import inside function, duplicate datetime alias, in-function logging re-imports, unnecessary json alias.
findings.analysis: Complexity and language agents independently flagged overlapping import issues across multiple files.
findings.suggested_approach: Remove redundant in-function imports; use module-level imports consistently.

findings.title: normalize_location defined but never called anywhere
findings.description: normalize_location function and 21-entry _LOCATION_CANONICAL dict have zero call sites. Docstring notes 'NOT used in dedup_key'.
findings.analysis: Complexity agent grep'd for normalize_location across all .py files and found only the definition, no call sites.
findings.suggested_approach: Remove normalize_location() and _LOCATION_CANONICAL. ~30 lines of dead code.

findings.title: purge_jobs.py is a one-shot migration script hardcoded to a past event
findings.description: 374-line standalone script hardcoded to specific bulk-load timestamp from March 2026. Once executed, always reports 'No spike found'. No future utility.
findings.analysis: Complexity agent identified the hardcoded BULK_TS sentinel and verified the script's one-shot nature.
findings.suggested_approach: Archive or remove. Future purge scripts should be parameterized.

findings.title: Heuristic JobScorer persists in ingestion despite AI scoring superseding it
findings.description: Heuristic scorer runs every ingestion but scores are immediately superseded by Haiku scoring. Only provides value during brief interval between upsert and Haiku completion.
findings.analysis: Design agent traced the scoring sequence in pipeline_runner and identified that COALESCE(sonnet_score, haiku_score, score) means heuristic score is last-resort fallback.
findings.suggested_approach: Evaluate maintenance cost vs value. If kept for offline/API-unavailable scenarios, document explicitly.

findings.title: Form elements missing associated labels in filter bar
findings.description: Multiple input and select elements in jobs filter bar lack associated label elements, reducing accessibility for screen readers.
findings.analysis: Language agent audited template accessibility and identified missing labels on filter bar form elements.
findings.suggested_approach: Add visually-hidden labels using Tailwind's sr-only class to preserve compact design while providing screen reader accessibility.
